title: Selective Isolation of Lipid Classes from Cold-Water Corals via SPE for Quantitative Fatty Acid Profiling
author: Audrey M. Pruski

steps.title: Sample Preparation
steps.instruction: Sample Preparation

steps.title: Flash-freeze Sample on board to prevent degradation
steps.instruction: Flash-freeze Sample on board to prevent degradation

steps.title: Freeze-dry samples and store at -80 °C until analysis
steps.instruction: Freeze-dry samples and store at -80 °C until analysis

steps.title: Grind freeze-dried Sample into a fine...
steps.instruction: Grind freeze-dried Sample into a fine powder using a bead mill homogenizer while keeping the chamber filled with liquid nitrogen to prevent heating

steps.title: Preparation of Working Solutions
steps.instruction: Preparation of Working Solutions

steps.title: Chloroform/methanol/water (1:2:0
steps.instruction: Chloroform/methanol/water (1:2:0.8) reagent: 5.265 mL CHCl₃, 10.525 mL CH 3 OH and 4.21 mL H₂O. Keep at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator

steps.title: 1M NaCl solution in ultrapure water (58g
steps.instruction: 1M NaCl solution in ultrapure water (58g.L -1 ). Keep at 4 °C in a refrigerator
0.3N NaOH solution (12 g.L-1). Keep at 4 °C in a refrigerator
0.2M H₃PO₄ solution (19.6g.L-1). Keep at 4 °C in a refrigerator

steps.title: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene) at 50 mg
steps.instruction: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene) at 50 mg.mL-1 in Chloroform. Keep at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator

steps.title: Total lipid extraction Total lipids are...
steps.instruction: Total lipid extraction Total lipids are extracted using a modified version of the Bligh and Dyer method (1959), optimized to improve extraction efficiency.

steps.title: Place the freeze-dried Sample (~ 3...
steps.instruction: Place the freeze-dried Sample (~ 3 g ) in a 30 mL glass tube (round bottom, screw tap with PTFE liner)

steps.title: Add 20 mL of CHCl₃/CH₃OH/H 2 O (1:2:0
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL of CHCl₃/CH₃OH/H 2 O (1:2:0.8) with 1M NaCl + 0.2M H₃PO₄ The solution of NaCl 1M-H 3 PO 4 0.2M increases protein unwinding and lipid recovery

steps.title: Sonicate 00:05:00 ( On ice, if...
steps.instruction: Sonicate 00:05:00 ( On ice, if needed ), then rotate 00:20:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 mL CHCl₃, then 2.5 mL H₂O. Shake 00:05:00

steps.title: 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Recover the organic phase (bottom) using a Pasteur pipette

steps.title: Repeat extraction 3 times with 5 mL CHCl₃
steps.instruction: Repeat extraction 3 times with 5 mL CHCl₃

steps.title: Pool all organic phases (final volume ≈ 22
steps.instruction: Pool all organic phases (final volume ≈ 22.765 mL) and vortex. ⚠ Keep organic phase cold On ice

steps.title: Wash organic phase with 2
steps.instruction: Wash organic phase with 2.5 mL cold 0.3N NaOH. Shake 00:01:00

steps.title: 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes...
steps.instruction: 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes and recover the organic phase

steps.title: Take 20 mL or more into...
steps.instruction: Take 20 mL or more into a new glass tube (conical bottom, screw tap with PTFE liner) and evaporate at 40 °C under vacuum (rotary evaporator) ⚠ Lipid extracts should never be allowed to dry out!

steps.title: Cool at -20 °C and redissolve...
steps.instruction: Cool at -20 °C and redissolve in 400 µL CHCl₃ + BHT (50 mg.L -1 ) BHT is added optionally to chloroform to prevent lipid oxidation during extraction!

steps.title: Centrifuge 2000 rpm, 4 °C, 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 2000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes Store at -20 °C

steps.title: Solvent Preparation
steps.instruction: Solvent Preparation

steps.title: Solvent A
steps.instruction: Solvent A: Chloroform:2-propanol (2:1 v/v) Solvent B: 2% Acetic acid in diethyl ether Solvent C: Methanol Solvent D: Hexane Solvent E: 1% Diethyl ether + 10% Dichloromethane in hexane Solvent F: 5% Ethyl acetate in hexane Solvent G: 15% Ethyl acetate in hexane Solvent H: Chloroform:Methanol (2:1 v/v) Keep all solvents at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator!

steps.title: Separation of neutral lipids, fatty acids...
steps.instruction: Separation of neutral lipids, fatty acids and phospholipids 3 Strata NH₂ (aminopropyl silica) cartridges (Phenomenex) are needed per sample. 7 lipid fractions are efficiently recovered from the initial chloroform extract with minimal impurities.

steps.title: Condition the first cartridge (C1) with...
steps.instruction: Condition the first cartridge (C1) with 2 × 2 mL hexane, vacuum dry 2–5 min

steps.title: Load 390 µL lipid extract
steps.instruction: Load 390 µL lipid extract. Vacuum to dryness

steps.title: Elute neutral lipids (E1)
steps.instruction: Elute neutral lipids (E1): 4 × 2 mL solvent A

steps.title: Elute free fatty acids (E2)
steps.instruction: Elute free fatty acids (E2): 4 × 2 mL solvent B

steps.title: Elute phospholipids (E3)
steps.instruction: Elute phospholipids (E3): 4 × 2 mL solvent C

steps.title: Store eluates at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store eluates at -20 °C .

steps.title: Neutral Lipid Fractionation In the original...
steps.instruction: Neutral Lipid Fractionation In the original method by Kaluzny et al. (1985), the elution of steryl esters was demonstrated using commercially available lipid standards, which did not include wax esters. However, based on our experimental results, wax esters appear to co-elute with steryl esters under the same solvent conditions used for fraction E4.

steps.title: Condition a second cartridge (C2) as above with hexane
steps.instruction: Condition a second cartridge (C2) as above with hexane

steps.title: Load 390 µL from E1
steps.instruction: Load 390 µL from E1. No need to dry

steps.title: Elute wax esters and sterol esters (E4)
steps.instruction: Elute wax esters and sterol esters (E4): 4 × 2 mL solvent D Note: Mono-, di-, and triglycerides and sterols are retained on C2.

steps.title: Condition a third cartridge (C3) as above
steps.instruction: Condition a third cartridge (C3) as above

steps.title: Stack C2 onto C3 using an adapter
steps.instruction: Stack C2 onto C3 using an adapter

steps.title: Elute triglycerides (E5)
steps.instruction: Elute triglycerides (E5): 6 × 2 mL solvent E

steps.title: Elute sterols (E6)
steps.instruction: Elute sterols (E6): 9 × 2 mL solvent F

steps.title: Remove and discard C3, place C2 back on manifold
steps.instruction: Remove and discard C3, place C2 back on manifold

steps.title: Elute diglycerides (E7)
steps.instruction: Elute diglycerides (E7): 4 × 2 mL solvent G

steps.title: Elute monoglycerides (E8)
steps.instruction: Elute monoglycerides (E8): 4 × 2 mL solvent H

steps.title: Evaporation of lipid fractions
steps.instruction: Evaporation of lipid fractions

steps.title: Evaporate under vacuum all eluates ( 45 °C )
steps.instruction: Evaporate under vacuum all eluates ( 45 °C )

steps.title: Redissolve in 100 µL CHCl₃ + BHT
steps.instruction: Redissolve in 100 µL CHCl₃ + BHT. Store at -20 °C Eluates for GC-MS analysis of fatty acids are E2 (Free Fatty Acids), E3 (Phospholipids), E4 (Wax esters and Sterol esters), E5 (Triglycerides), E7 (Diglycerides), and E8 (Monoglycerides). E6 may be used for sterol analysis!

steps.title: Preparation of Working Solutions - Transmethylation reagent
steps.instruction: Preparation of Working Solutions - Transmethylation reagent: 3.4 mL Methanol + 0.6 mL H₂SO₄ (98%), and 4.0 mL CHCl₃ with BHT (50 mg/L). Keep at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator Reagent should be cold and freshly prepared - C19:0 (internal standard) stock solution ~1 mg.mL ‑1 in hexane. The solution is diluted before use (1/9). Keep at -20 °C Adjust the concentration of the internal standard so that its peak on the chromatogram falls within the range of peak intensities, ideally between the most abundant and the least abundant fatty acids. This ensures accurate quantification across the entire dynamic range

steps.title: To the eluates
steps.instruction: To the eluates: add 20 µL internal standard C19:0 + 8 mL of transmethylation reagent. Cap and shake

steps.title: Incubate 01:30:00 at 90 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate 01:30:00 at 90 °C . Shake occasionally (3-4 times)

steps.title: Cool ( On ice or -20 °C )
steps.instruction: Cool ( On ice or -20 °C ). Open carefully

steps.title: Recover lower organic phase in a new glass tube
steps.instruction: Recover lower organic phase in a new glass tube

steps.title: Organic Phase Wash
steps.instruction: Organic Phase Wash

steps.title: Lipid extraction and Transesterification
steps.instruction: Lipid extraction and Transesterification

steps.title: Phase Separation
steps.instruction: Phase Separation

steps.title: Add 2 mL cold H₂O
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL cold H₂O. Vortex. 300 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes Centrifuge 5 min, 3000 rpm, 4°C

steps.title: Fatty acid methyl extracts are ready...
steps.instruction: Fatty acid methyl extracts are ready for GC-MS analysis In this study, the analyses were carried out using the instrumental conditions described in a companion protocol developed by our group (Pruski & Vétion, 2024).

steps.title: Transfer to GC vials with a 200 µl insert
steps.instruction: Transfer to GC vials with a 200 µl insert. Store at -80 °C

steps.title: Redissolve in 30-50 µL pure hexane
steps.instruction: Redissolve in 30-50 µL pure hexane. Vortex, 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Evaporate solvent in a SpeedVac concentrator at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Evaporate solvent in a SpeedVac concentrator at Room temperature . Cool at -20 °C

steps.title: Discard the upper polar layer and...
steps.instruction: Discard the upper polar layer and transfer 6-9 mL of the lower organic phase to a clean screw-cap tube

steps.title: Wash with 4 mL cold 2% K₂CO₃
steps.instruction: Wash with 4 mL cold 2% K₂CO₃. 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Combine all organic phases (~8–9 mL)
steps.instruction: Combine all organic phases (~8–9 mL)

steps.title: Re-extract aqueous phase 2 times with...
steps.instruction: Re-extract aqueous phase 2 times with 2 mL cold hexane:CHCl₃ (4:1)

steps.title: Final Purification
steps.instruction: Final Purification

steps.title: Drying and Dissolving FAMEs
steps.instruction: Drying and Dissolving FAMEs
